description: HDAC isoform-specific IC50 values (nM) for comp-007 candidates. Sourced from ChEMBL v34 and PubMed primary literature. All values are for human recombinant enzymes unless noted. Values marked 'null' indicate data unavailable (Stage 1 gap). Values marked as estimates use mechanistic extrapolation from cellular assays.
data_quality_note: Heterogeneous assay formats across compounds (biochemical recombinant vs. nuclear extract vs. cellular). Direct comparison is approximate. Butyrate data (ChEMBL) is biochemical recombinant — highest confidence for ranking. Garlic-compound data is nuclear-extract cellular — moderate confidence. Sulforaphane data is semi-quantitative activity assay — lower confidence. Caffeic/ferulic acid: no isoform-specific IC50 available.

entries.HDAC1_source: ChEMBL activity_id 5207850; assay CHEMBL1680132 — Inhibition of human recombinant HDAC1; document CHEMBL1671826, ACS Med Chem Lett 2011. Biochemical assay, single protein format.
entries.HDAC2_source: ChEMBL activity_id 5207851; assay CHEMBL1680133 — human recombinant HDAC2; ACS Med Chem Lett 2011.
entries.HDAC3_source: ChEMBL activity_id 5207852; assay CHEMBL1680134 — human recombinant HDAC3; ACS Med Chem Lett 2011.
entries.HDAC6_source: ChEMBL activity_id 5207858; assay CHEMBL1680140 — human recombinant HDAC6; ACS Med Chem Lett 2011. Marked 'Outside typical range'; effectively no HDAC6 inhibition.
entries.notes: Butyrate shows excellent class I selectivity: HDAC6 IC50 >2 mM vs HDAC1-3 IC50 9-16 uM — selectivity ratio >125x. Canonical food-grade HDACi benchmark for Q141K rescue (Basseville 2012 PMID 22472121 used class I HDACi vorinostat as reference; butyrate at 1 mM rescues Q141K trafficking ~30-50%).

entries.HDAC1_source: No isoform-specific IC50 in ChEMBL (89 total activities; none against HDAC1/2/3/6). Cellular data: SFN decreases HDAC1, HDAC2, HDAC3 protein expression in JB6 skin cells (PMID 24441674, Su 2014 Cancer Prev Res). This is protein downregulation, not direct enzymatic inhibition — different mechanism from butyrate.
entries.HDAC2_source: Semi-quantitative: Choi 2018 (PMID 29482060, Biomed Pharmacother) reports 'Inhibition of HDAC2 activity by sulforaphane was stronger than piceatannol' at 1-10 uM in smooth muscle cell nuclear extracts — qualitative, no IC50 reported. Stronger than class IIa inhibition by sulforaphane.
entries.HDAC3_source: No direct HDAC3 IC50 data. Su 2014 (PMID 24441674) shows HDAC3 protein downregulation at 5-10 uM SFN in JB6 cells.
entries.HDAC6_source: Choi 2018 (PMID 29482060): sulforaphane shows 'mild inhibition of class IIa HDACs' (HDAC4/5/7/9) but class IIb (HDAC6) not directly tested. Weak class IIa effect implies low HDAC6 inhibition — favorable selectivity profile inferred.
entries.notes: SFN HDAC inhibition is primarily indirect: HDAC protein level downregulation (transcriptional/epigenetic reprogramming) rather than direct enzymatic inhibition. This is a different mechanism from butyrate's zinc chelation. No usable IC50 for ranking formula. Gut concentration is low (5-20 uM achievable). Stage 1 gap: no isoform IC50 available.
entries.effective_HDAC_class_I_IC50_estimate_bas: Mechanistic extrapolation from cellular HDAC activity inhibition at 5-10 uM SFN (Nian 2009 PMID 19197985 — HDAC activity in PBMC reduced by ~40% after broccoli sprout ingestion; Myzak 2006 prostate data). Estimated effective IC50 ~5 uM = 5000 nM. Use for ranking only with LOW confidence flag.
entries.HDAC6_selectivity_estimated: favorable — no direct HDAC6 inhibition reported at dietary-achievable concentrations

entries.HDAC1_source: No ChEMBL entry. Primary literature: Nian 2009 (PMID 19197985) describes AM as 'competitive HDAC inhibitor' in human colon cancer cells. Druesne 2004 (PMID 14976134) shows 92% HDAC activity inhibition at 200 uM AM in Caco-2 nuclear extracts. No isoform-resolved IC50.
entries.HDAC2_source: Same as HDAC1 — bulk HDAC activity inhibition only, no isoform resolution.
entries.HDAC6_source: No data. Thiol-zinc chelation mechanism similar to butyrate carboxylate zinc chelation — mechanistic extrapolation suggests HDAC6 may also be inhibited (thiol is a stronger zinc chelator than carboxylate). This is a concern — HDAC6 inhibition not ruled out.
entries.notes: 92% HDAC activity inhibition at 200 uM in nuclear extract (Druesne 2004). Estimated bulk IC50 ~20-50 uM (interpolating from 29% at 200 uM DADS and 92% at 200 uM AM — AM is much more potent). Thiol mechanism may not spare HDAC6. Stage 1 gap: isoform selectivity and HDAC6 data absent.
entries.effective_HDAC_class_I_IC50_estimate_bas: Estimated from 92% activity inhibition at 200 uM in mixed nuclear extract (Druesne 2004 PMID 14976134). Using Hill equation n=1: 92% inhibition = ratio/(ratio+1) → ratio ~11.5 → IC50 ~200/11.5 = ~17 uM = ~17,000 nM. Conservative estimate ~50,000 nM used to account for nuclear extract overestimation vs recombinant.
entries.HDAC6_selectivity_estimated: unknown — thiol mechanism raises concern for HDAC6 co-inhibition; flag as Stage 1 gap

entries.HDAC1_source: No ChEMBL entry. Druesne 2004 (PMID 14976134): 200 uM DADS decreases HDAC activity by 29% in Caco-2 nuclear extracts. No isoform-specific IC50. Less potent than its metabolite AM.
entries.HDAC6_source: No data. DADS is a prodrug for AM; HDAC6 risk inherited from AM uncertainty.
entries.notes: DADS is a dietary precursor — its HDACi activity in gut lumen is primarily via conversion to allyl mercaptan. Direct DADS HDAC inhibition is weaker than AM (29% vs 92% at 200 uM). For gut lumen purposes, DADS and AM are partially overlapping. Stage 1 gap: no isoform IC50.
entries.effective_HDAC_class_I_IC50_estimate_bas: 29% inhibition at 200 uM in nuclear extract → Hill equation: 29% = ratio/(ratio+1) → ratio = 0.41 → IC50 = 200/0.41 = 488 uM = ~500,000-1,000,000 nM. Conservative 1 mM estimate as DADS acts mainly as AM prodrug.

entries.HDAC1_source: No ChEMBL entry for PEITC as HDAC inhibitor. PMID 31228495 (Gupta 2019): PEITC reduces HDAC1 levels in colorectal cancer model in vivo (rat) and reduces DNMT1 and HDAC1 protein in HT-29 cells. Cellular IC50 for HT-29 growth inhibition = 11.88 uM. No isolated HDAC1 IC50.
entries.HDAC6_source: No data. Isothiocyanate metabolism via mercapturic acid pathway; may share HDAC inhibition mechanism with SFN but HDAC6 selectivity uncharacterized.
entries.notes: PEITC HDACi evidence is cell-level protein downregulation (HDAC1 protein) not biochemical IC50. Mechanism likely similar to SFN — indirect, via transcriptional/epigenetic reprogramming. Stage 1 gap: no usable IC50 data.
entries.effective_HDAC_class_I_IC50_estimate_bas: Estimated from SFN analogy (same isothiocyanate class, similar molecular structure). SFN estimated ~5,000 nM; PEITC slightly weaker at cellular level (cell IC50 11.88 uM vs SFN typically 3-5 uM in comparable assays). Estimated 10,000 nM. LOW confidence — analogical extrapolation only.
entries.HDAC6_selectivity_estimated: unknown — isothiocyanate; likely similar to SFN (mild class IIa effect, HDAC6 unclear)

entries.HDAC1_source: ChEMBL: 192 activities for CHEMBL145; none against HDAC1, HDAC2, HDAC3, or HDAC6 (confirmed by ChEMBL query 2026-05-05). PubMed: no isoform-specific HDAC IC50 found for caffeic acid per se (PMID 27588384 covers related phenolics — ferulic acid, p-coumaric acid — but not caffeic acid directly).
entries.notes: Caffeic acid has documented NF-kB pathway effects and cellular HDAC-related outcomes (H3 acetylation), but no published isoform-specific IC50 for HDAC1/2/3/6. The catechol moiety can chelate zinc (theoretical mechanism), but no quantitative validation. Stage 1 gap: no usable IC50 data.

entries.HDAC1_source: ChEMBL: 249 activities for CHEMBL32749; none against HDAC1/2/3/6 (confirmed 2026-05-05). PMID 27588384 (Saenglee 2016, Pharmacol Rep): ferulic acid causes H3 hyperacetylation in MCF-7 and HeLa cells and inhibits HDAC activity in MTT/Western context, but no IC50 for specific isoforms reported.
entries.notes: Ferulic acid shows histone H3 hyperacetylation consistent with HDAC inhibition (PMID 27588384), but no quantitative IC50 for any HDAC isoform is available. Methoxy substitution on catechol reduces metal-chelation capacity vs caffeic acid. Stage 1 gap: no usable IC50 data.
